title: Mini Dictionary Side Pane on ERD
story: As a DataTools Pro user exploring the ERD, I want to click on an object node and see a simplified dictionary view in a side pane without leaving the ERD page, so that I can quickly reference field details while analyzing entity relationships.
description:
Adds a side pane to the ERD page that displays a simplified dictionary view when a user clicks the launch icon on an object node. The side pane shows field name, label, and an edit link for each field — a lightweight version of the full dictionary page.

The side pane reuses existing dictionary page code where possible, with the Action column suppressed. A Field Name filter is provided within the side pane for searching. When records exceed the visible area, the side pane table scrolls independently.

This feature is designed to keep users on the ERD page while giving them quick access to field-level details, supporting the trend of users interacting with the ERD more than the standalone dictionary.
acceptance_criteria: Clicking the launch icon on an ERD object node opens a right-side pane
The side pane displays field name, label, and edit link for each field of the selected object
A Field Name filter is available within the side pane
The Action column from the full dictionary view is suppressed in the side pane
The side pane table scrolls independently when records exceed the visible area
Existing dictionary page code is reused where possible
The side pane can be closed to return to the full ERD view
ux_changes: New right-side pane on the ERD page that opens on object node click, displaying a simplified dictionary table with field name, label, and edit link. Includes a Field Name filter and independent scroll for long field lists.
middleware_changes: GoJS node template updated to add a click handler that triggers the side pane. Existing dictionary rendering code abstracted and reused in the side pane context.
implementation_notes: Estimated at 8 hours. Required modifying GoJS node code to add the side pane trigger, reusing existing dictionary UI components, and implementing a Field Name filter. The Action column was suppressed per discussion. The feature was positioned as a good onboarding task for a new developer since it requires understanding both the dictionary and ERD subsystems.
comment_summary: Impact analysis estimated 8 hours. Developer worked through GoJS integration, suppressed Action column per discussion, and added a Field Name filter in the side pane. A brief blocker was resolved by discussing the single-filter approach. Side pane table includes independent scrollbar for large datasets.
